statements.__comment: Project `_ont_enabled` on every EntraApplication from the same-tenant EntraServicePrincipal. Microsoft Graph exposes `accountEnabled` on the service principal (tenant-local instance) rather than on the application registration. The SERVICE_PRINCIPAL relationship is matched only on `app_id`, so a multi-tenant Entra graph can attach service principals from foreign tenants to a home-tenant application; selecting the service principal through the shared EntraTenant guarantees we read the home-tenant state and that writes are deterministic. OPTIONAL MATCH guarantees every application is touched: when an app has no service principal in its own tenant (e.g. the SP was just deleted by cleanup), `_ont_enabled` is reset to NULL so stale values from prior syncs cannot linger.
statements.query: MATCH (app:EntraApplication)<-[:RESOURCE]-(tenant:EntraTenant) OPTIONAL MATCH (tenant)-[:RESOURCE]->(sp:EntraServicePrincipal {app_id: app.app_id}) WITH app, sp SET app._ont_enabled = sp.account_enabled
